plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs to eliminate that risk.
Significantly trim broad index and semiconductor exposure.
Increase allocation to the income-focused ETF.
Add a top-rated technology stock for quality exposure.
This plan aims for capital preservation with modest growth.
Expect lower volatility and drawdowns.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation, aiming for around 1.5% monthly return. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, holding a larger cash buffer. Expect lower volatility and drawdowns compared to your current book.

plan: Reduce leveraged ETF exposure to a minimal level.
Trim broad index and semiconductor holdings.
Increase allocation to the top-rated technology stock.
Add another strong technology name for diversification.
This plan balances growth with risk reduction.
It aims for steady, risk-first growth.
balanced.read: This plan seeks risk-first growth, targeting around 2.5% monthly return. It trims leverage and concentration while adding high-quality tech names. Expect moderate growth with controlled drawdowns.

plan: Increase allocation to the 3x semiconductor ETF.
Trim broad index and semiconductor holdings.
Concentrate further into the highest-scoring tech names.
Add an inverse semiconductor ETF for hedging.
This plan aggressively pursues growth through leverage and concentration.
Expect higher potential returns and significantly larger drawdowns.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for substantial growth, targeting around 4% monthly return, but with significantly increased risk. It leans heavily into leveraged ETFs and concentrated tech positions, accepting higher potential drawdowns and the effects of leverage decay. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Eliminate all leveraged and high-beta single name positions.
Significantly trim broad index exposure.
Maximize allocation to the income-focused ETF.
Add a stable dividend-paying stock.
This plan prioritizes generating consistent monthly income.
It aims for very low drawdowns and reliable distributions.
income.read: This plan focuses on generating steady monthly income, targeting around 1% monthly return. It shifts the portfolio towards income-generating assets and stable dividend payers, minimizing volatility and growth potential. Distributions are variable and NAV can still decline.
